tickets.title: Primitive byte arrays with a MIME type: a bytes Target kind (0.4, additive)
tickets.body:
## Proposal

Add an **additive** `Target` kind for raw binary payloads with an attached MIME type:

```ts
{ kind: "bytes", mime: string, value: Uint8Array }
```

- **Identity is the hash of the raw bytes.** Encoding is transport, never identity: base64url in JSON wire contexts, a native byte string in CBOR. This removes base64's canonicalization hazards (variant alphabets, padding) from the content address entirely.
- **MIME rides in-kind**, RDF-datatype style ŌĆö attested interpretation-testimony that travels with the bytes, not a detachable sibling pointer. Two claims asserting the same bytes under different MIME types are different claims, which is honest.

## Motivation

Loam is converging on a doctrine of *code and documents shipped as deltas*: a delta asserts the content-addressed bytes of a coherent, versioned unit, and the signature attests exactly the bytes that run (Loam TODO, "The snapshot doctrine"). Text (ESM source) works today as a string primitive. But the renderer work coming in Loam ┬¦23 ŌĆö UI components pushed as deltas, mounted by a stock host ŌĆö wants **basic binary assets right in the hypergraph**: icons, small images, fonts, wasm. Today those mean base64-in-a-string: ~4/3 bloat, no typing, and an encoding sitting inside the content address.

A `bytes` primitive also gives every downstream surface what it needs: a REST door can answer with a correct `Content-Type`, an inspector can render "a 43 KB `image/png`" instead of a base64 wall, and sandbox/quarantine machinery gets a signal about what kind of thing it is holding.

## Compatibility

Additive, so the story is clean:

- **No migration.** Existing deltas do not change; old stores simply never contain the new kind. The new shape is unambiguously distinct from all prior versions by construction (a new `kind`), so shape-detection can never misfire.
- **Federation forward-compat**: a pre-0.4 peer pulling from a store holding `bytes` deltas refuses the unknown kind. That is version discipline, not breakage ŌĆö and admission shape-requirements can express it explicitly.

## Non-goals / cautions

- **This is not a blob store.** A 10 MB image in a delta stresses every path ŌĆö hyperview copies, federation offers, a browser peer's ~5 MB origin quota. The primitive is the *inline* rung of a storage ladder (inline bytes ŌåÆ content-addressed ref ŌåÆ chunked tree); large assets still climb the ladder, and doors may cap sizes as deployment config. The primitive's job is to make the inline rung honest and the reference rungs typed.
- The delta grammar stays small; this adds one kind, not a media subsystem.

## Open questions for the design

1. Exact canonical form: where the raw bytes enter the hash preimage, and the pinned JSON-wire encoding (base64url, unpadded?).
2. Conformance vectors: hash-stability vectors for the new kind across the TS and Rust implementations.
3. Any size guidance the substrate itself should state (likely none ŌĆö a caps-are-deployment-config note may be enough).
4. Whether `mime` is required or defaults to `application/octet-stream`.

Proposed as rhizomatic 0.4, timed alongside Loam's ┬¦23 (renderers) design stage; nothing in Loam ┬¦21/┬¦22 blocks on it.

­¤ż¢ Drafted with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) at Myk's request.

tickets.title: Add a Schema Schema (`SCHEMA_SCHEMA`) ŌĆö self-hosting parity for the resolution Schema
tickets.body:
rhizomatic ships `HYPER_SCHEMA_SCHEMA` ŌĆö the self-hosting definition of a **HyperSchema** ŌĆö plus `publishSchemaClaims`/`loadSchema` to publish and load one as deltas. There is **no equivalent for the resolution `Schema`** (`{ props: Map<field, Policy>, default: Policy }`): no `SCHEMA_SCHEMA`, no at-rest delta vocabulary for a Schema, and the `Schema` type carries no name. A Schema today exists only inline (canonical JSON quoted inside a consumer's registration), never as a first-class, resolvable domain node.

**Why this is needed.** Loam's ┬¦21 (schema identity & versioning) makes a Schema a first-class entity ŌĆö its own id, its own deltas, resolved from the ground exactly as a HyperSchema is ŌĆö with registration demoted to a binding, so that many named lenses can live over one HyperSchema and each can be snapshotted into a pinnable `name@hash` version. That requires publishing/loading a Schema *as deltas, resolved by a Schema Schema*. The whole system rests on the symmetry `HyperSchema : HyperView :: Schema : View`; if one side of it has a self-hosting schema-schema and publish/load, the other side deserves the parallel for the identical reasons ŌĆö self-description, portability, resolvability. Doing it in Loam instead would fork the substrate's self-hosting story at the application layer (a parallel `loam.schema.*`), exactly the near-synonym duplication the vocabulary discipline avoids.

**Proposed.** Add `SCHEMA_SCHEMA` (the self-hosting definition of a resolution Schema) and publish/load parity ŌĆö either `publishSchemaClaims`/`loadSchema` equivalents for Schemas, or a generalization of the existing pair ŌĆö with an at-rest delta vocabulary (`rhizomatic.schema.*`, parallel to `rhizomatic.hyperschema.*`).

**Coordination.** This blocks Loam ┬¦21, which already ships an on-wire migration (its `schema:`ŌåÆ`hyperschema:` entity rename). Ideally this lands in the **same release** as the naming reconciliation in #10, so Loam pulls one new rhizomatic and folds everything into ┬¦21's single migration wave rather than cutting a second one.

**A deeper question, noted not blocked (Myk).** Whether the query/resolution apparatus (schema-schemas, Policies, `resolveView`) is fundamentally substrate, or a separable layer above the truly-portable data format (deltas, content-addressing, signatures, negation), is a real long-horizon architectural question ŌĆö one could imagine the format being the substrate and the query layer living above it. But the HyperSchema Schema already lives in rhizomatic, so for consistency the Schema Schema joining it is the smaller, parity-preserving move; the separability question can be its own discussion.

Referenced by Loam ┬¦21 (schema identity); related to #10 (the `loadSchema`/`publishSchemaClaims` naming lag).

tickets.title: Vocabulary: `loadSchema` / `publishSchemaClaims` operate on HyperSchemas, not Schemas
tickets.body:
Since the 0.3.0 L5 realignment, the at-rest schema-definition vocabulary is `rhizomatic.hyperschema.*`, and the conceptual split is **HyperSchema** (the gather program) vs **Schema** (the resolution program: `{ props: Map<field, Policy>, default: Policy }`). But the public API `loadSchema` and `publishSchemaClaims` load and publish **HyperSchemas** ŌĆö the names predate the realignment and now lag their meaning.

This surfaced while designing Loam's ┬¦21 (schema identity & versioning), which makes a Loam-level Schema a first-class entity distinct from the HyperSchema. Loam is renaming its own `schema:` entity prefix to remove the blur on its side, but the substrate API names remain a source of confusion: a reader of `loadSchema` reasonably expects it to load a Schema (a resolution program), when it in fact loads a HyperSchema (a gather program).

rhizomatic is frozen/normative, so this is filed for discussion, not as a Loam workaround. Options:
- **(a)** rename to `loadHyperSchema` / `publishHyperSchemaClaims` ŌĆö breaking API change (conformance vectors + version bump + migration guidance);
- **(b)** add the accurate names as aliases and deprecate the old ones;
- **(c)** document the lag and keep the names as stable API even though the vocabulary moved under them.

No urgency ŌĆö Loam routes around it in prose today (┬¦21 records the seam). Raising per Myk's request to resolve the last naming detail.

_Filed from the Loam ┬¦21 design review._

edges.contract: the settled loadSchema/publishSchemaClaims API naming that #11 designs its Schema publish/load parity against
